symptoms.symptom_name: Pain out of proportion, refractory to strong opioids
Severe pain on passive toe movement

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Highly specific sign of ischaemic muscle within the compartment.

symptoms.symptom_name: New paraesthesia over the dorsum of the foot

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reflects early nerve ischaemia within the affected compartment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent tibial fracture with rigid cast application

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rigid cast over a swelling fracture is a classic precipitant.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tense/shiny swollen leg, delayed capillary refill, early toe extension weakness, preserved distal pulses

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective confirmation; clinician-only findings sealed from telehealth chat. Preserved pulses do not exclude the diagnosis.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Opioid-refractory pain out of proportion, pain on passive stretch, and new paraesthesia after cast application together confirm acute compartment syndrome requiring emergency cast removal.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate cast bivalving/removal and intracompartmental pressure measurement in the Emergency Department
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms diagnosis and is often therapeutic in itself if done early enough.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent orthopaedic surgical review for possible fasciotomy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive treatment if pressures remain elevated after cast removal.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Increasing the oxycodone dose and reviewing again in the morning
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Would dangerously delay treatment of a limb-threatening surgical emergency.
